shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, south side of Arbiter's Grounds
shrines.oneLine: No puzzle inside; reach the altar at Arbiter's Grounds and claim the free Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Motsusis Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, inside South Lomei Labyrinth
shrines.oneLine: Reward for solving the maze: follow the acorn/tree-nut trail through the labyrinth to reach this shrine.

shrines.name: Otutsum Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, west of Gerudo Summit on Risoka Snowfield
shrines.oneLine: No challenge inside; open the chest (Topaz) and take the free Light of Blessing from the altar.

shrines.name: Suariwak Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, Taafei Hill Cave near the Yiga Blademaster Station
shrines.oneLine: Reward shrine unlocked after the Yiga Clan exam; reach the cave and claim the free Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rakakudaj Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Canyon / Highlands, east of Koukot Plateau (Gerudo Canyon Path)
shrines.oneLine: Solve the Gerudo Canyon Crystal quest: carry the green crystal to the laser endpoint to unlock the shrine.

shrines.name: Turakamik Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, north of Gerudo Canyon Mine
shrines.oneLine: Fuse the powered metal ball to the other, Recall the cogwheel to ride it up, then Ascend to the altar.

shrines.name: Kitawak Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, East Gerudo Mesa northeast of the Desert Labyrinth
shrines.oneLine: Ultrahand stone slabs into bridges and ramps, then ride the piston-launched catapult up to the altar.

shrines.name: Mayamats Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, on Rutimala Hill
shrines.oneLine: Tilt and steer the rolling ball along the course, using Ultrahand to build its path to the goal.

shrines.name: Rotsumamu Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, gully between Vatorsa Snowfield and Sapphia's Table
shrines.oneLine: Attach the heavy barrel/block to the seesaw's far side so you can walk up the balanced ramp to the door.

shrines.name: Mayasiar Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands Sky, Starview Island
shrines.oneLine: Reach it via the light/rocket puzzle on Starview Island; the altar itself is a free Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rakashog Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands Sky, East Gerudo Sky Archipelago
shrines.oneLine: Aim the movable mirror to bounce light onto the receivers, opening the way through the sky shrine.

shrines.name: Siyamotsus Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands Sky, top floor of South Lomei Castle
shrines.oneLine: Light the side torches on the rising structure to reveal a trampoline that launches you up to the altar.

shrineRegions.notes: 15 shrines: 10 Surface (Hebra Mountains) + 5 Hebra Sky. Names, count, subtitles, categories, and shrine quests cross-checked across SegmentNext, Game8, GGRecon, Gamerant, Shacknews, PowerPyx. Verified: Tauyosipun spelling is correct; Eutoum=Proving Grounds: Infiltration, Otak=Proving Grounds: Traps, Orochium=Courage to Fall, Oshozan-u=Mallet Smash, Wao-os=Lever Power, Sahirow=Aid from Above, Tauyosipun=Forward or Backward?, Ijo-o=More than Defense, Mayaumekis=Downward Force, Tenbez=Gravity and Velocity. FIXES: (1) Taninoud now includes shrineQuest 'The East Hebra Sky Crystal' (input wrongly had null and called it a free reward); it requires a crystal-carry quest. (2) Sahirow location corrected to atop/near Corvash Peak, east of Rospro Pass Skyview Tower (input said 'west of Corvash Peak'). CAVEAT: the Hebra/Tabantha boundary is disputed. This uses the common 15-shrine Hebra grouping (SegmentNext/Dot Esports/Game8 region map). GGRecon files ~27 shrines under Hebra, adding Gatakis, Nouda, Oromuwak, Makurukis, Iun-orok, Mayausiy, Gasas, Ikatak, Runakity, and the Ga-ahisas/Ganos/Taunhiy sky trio, which most guides assign to Tabantha (Frontier/Snowfield). If the app map puts those in Hebra, the count rises to ~27. Mineru/Spirit Temple and the four temple regions are elsewhere.

shrines.name: Eutoum Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Goflam's Secret Hot Spring, northern Hebra Mountains
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Infiltration. You start stripped of gear, so grab the weapons by the entrance, then sneak or fight past the Soldier Constructs.

shrines.name: Mayaotaki Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), at the center of North Lomei Labyrinth in North Tabantha Snowfield
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Solving the North Lomei Labyrinth maze to reach it is the trial; the shrine itself has no inner puzzle.

shrines.name: Orochium Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), near Snowfield Stable in South Tabantha Snowfield, northwest of the Forgotten Temple
shrines.oneLine: Courage to Fall. Step off the edge and ride the rising gusts of wind to drift safely down onto the lower platforms toward the exit.

shrines.name: Oshozan-u Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), atop an icy hill west of North Lomei Labyrinth
shrines.oneLine: Mallet Smash. Wield the big hammer with Ultrahand to smash blocks and pound the switches that open the way forward.

shrines.name: Otak Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Icefall Foothills Cave (melt the ice blocking the entrance with fire)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Traps. You start weaponless, so use the provided gear to fight Constructs while dodging the spike and floor traps.

shrines.name: Rutafu-um Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Hebra Mountains Northwest Cave, northwest of Hebra East Summit
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Do The Northwest Hebra Cave Crystal quest: carry the crystal through the cave to its pedestal to spawn the shrine.

shrines.name: Sahirow Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), atop Corvash Peak, east of Rospro Pass Skyview Tower, north of Rito Village
shrines.oneLine: Aid from Above. Use Ascend to phase up through the ceilings, rising past the laser nets to reach each higher level and the exit.

shrines.name: Sisuran Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), northwest of Pikida Stonegrove Skyview Tower, northeast of Hebra East Summit
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Do The North Hebra Mountains Crystal quest: beat the Frost Talus (Fire Arrows or Yunobo) and haul its crystal to the pedestal.

shrines.name: Tauyosipun Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), on the western slopes southwest of Hebra West Summit
shrines.oneLine: Forward or Backward? Use Recall to reverse moving platforms and rails so they carry you the direction you actually need to go.

shrines.name: Wao-os Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside West Lake Totori Cave, west of Rito Village
shrines.oneLine: Lever Power. Use Ultrahand with the lever-arm contraptions to apply force, swinging platforms or launching yourself across the gaps.

shrines.name: Ijo-o Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Sky, on the West Hebra Sky Archipelago (launch from Rospro Pass Skyview Tower)
shrines.oneLine: More than Defense. Use the big stone shield-plates as more than cover, angling them to block the flames and reach the next platform.

shrines.name: Kahatanaum Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Sky, on the Rising Island Chain northwest of the Wind Temple
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. The climb across the sky islands to reach it is the trial; the shrine inside has no puzzle, just the reward.

shrines.name: Mayaumekis Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Sky, on the Rising Island Chain on the way to the Wind Temple
shrines.oneLine: Downward Force. Jump onto the floating Zonai boats so their lift launches you upward, hopping ship to ship to the top.

shrines.name: Taninoud Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Sky, on the East Hebra Sky Archipelago (launch from Pikida Stonegrove Skyview Tower, glide northeast)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Do The East Hebra Sky Crystal quest: carry the crystal across the sky islands to its pedestal to spawn the shrine.

shrines.name: Tenbez Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Sky, at the North Lomei Castle Top Floor
shrines.oneLine: Gravity and Velocity. Drop the heavy balls and use Recall plus the slopes so momentum and gravity roll them onto the switches.

shrineRegions.notes: 10 shrines in the Tabantha Frontier map region: 8 Surface + 2 Sky. Roster, names, and categories cross-verified across miketendo64, segmentnext, Game8, Thonky, and Gamerant. No invented or missing shrines; all 10 names are real. FIXES vs prior draft: (1) Ga-ahisas's shrineQuest was wrongly listed as a quest ('Lightcast Island light/mirror puzzle') and the notes wrongly said BOTH Sky shrines are gated behind named quests. Ga-ahisas is gated by an ENVIRONMENTAL mirror puzzle on Lightcast Island, not a quest-log shrine quest, so its shrineQuest is null. Only Ganos has a named shrine quest ('The Tabantha Sky Crystal'). (2) Gatakis location corrected: it is reached by gliding north from Rospro Pass Skyview Tower, not via an unverified 'wind tunnel at a broken bridge.' (3) Iun-orok coords aligned to verified ~-3539, 0851, -0119. Ikatak's 'The Gisa Crater Crystal' quest confirmed. SCOPE CAVEAT: TotK's map treats 'Tabantha Frontier' and adjacent 'Hebra Mountains' as distinct regions; this is scoped to Tabantha Frontier proper. If 'tabantha' should also cover Hebra/Hebra Sky, those shrines must be added separately.

shrines.name: Gasas Shrine
shrines.location: Southwest of Tanagar Canyon, in the far southwest corner of Tabantha Frontier (approx -4153, 0098, 0055).
shrines.oneLine: Well-Timed Cuts: cut the rope to drop the big cube onto the ramp with an overhang, then Ascend up through it to cross the gap.

shrines.name: Gatakis Shrine
shrines.location: North of Rospro Pass Skyview Tower; launch from the tower and glide north to reach it (approx -3652, 1806, 0168).
shrines.oneLine: Ride the Winds: glide on the updrafts, weave through the laser gaps, and open your paraglider in the gusts to rise to each exit.

shrines.name: Ikatak Shrine
shrines.location: Southwestern Tabantha Frontier, just above Gisa Crater south of Rito Village (approx -3950, 1138, 0112).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle inside. Appears after the shrine quest. Open the chest and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Iun-orok Shrine
shrines.location: Underground inside Tanagar Canyon West Cave, in the valley below the canyon (approx -3539, 0851, -0119).
shrines.oneLine: The Right Roll: glue only TWO balls together (not three) so the orb rolls straight down the ramp and lands on the target.

shrines.name: Mayausiy Shrine
shrines.location: Deep inside the Forgotten Temple, north of Tanagar Canyon near Lindor's Brow (approx -1165, 2602, -0083).
shrines.oneLine: Building Blocks: use Ultrahand to grab and rotate the loose L-pieces so the incomplete square matches the finished model beside it.

shrines.name: Nouda Shrine
shrines.location: Inside Kopeeki Drifts Cave; ride a horse from Snowfield Stable to reach the snowy cave entrance (approx -2319, 2200, 0173).
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Intermediate. Stripped of your gear, grab the supplied weapons, fuse enemy parts onto them, and Ascend up to clear each tier.

shrines.name: Oromuwak Shrine
shrines.location: On a mountaintop southeast of the Lucky Clover Gazette, near Brightcap Cave (approx -3079, 1617, 0243).
shrines.oneLine: A Launching Device: fuse a Rocket to a minecart, point it up the rails, hop in and ignite it to ride up to the next area.

shrines.name: Turakawak Shrine
shrines.location: Northern Tabantha Frontier near Tabantha Hills, at the region's north edge (approx -3497, -0197, 0066).
shrines.oneLine: Stacking a Path: use Ultrahand to stack the climbable blocks into a tower against each ledge, then climb to reach the ladder and exit.

shrines.name: Ga-ahisas Shrine
shrines.location: On Lightcast Island in the North Tabantha Sky Archipelago; glide up using the sky-island launchers (approx -3596, 0962, 1699).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no inside puzzle. Solve the Lightcast Island mirror puzzle outside, redirecting the light beam to reveal the shrine.

shrines.name: Ganos Shrine
shrines.location: On an island south of Lightcast Island in the Tabantha Frontier Sky Archipelago (approx -3371, 0466, 1695).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle inside. Unlocked by the shrine quest; open the chest for a Diamond and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Makurukis Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, north of Tabantha Bridge Stable on a ledge of Mount Rhoam (approx -2847, 0630, 0233).
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Archery. Grab the bow by the door and headshot each construct. For the trio, Ascend a side pillar and shoot in bullet time as you fall.

shrines.name: Runakit Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, west of Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower between Upland Lindor and Mount Rhoam (approx -2531, 1170, 0178).
shrines.oneLine: Built to Carry. Use Ultrahand to attach stone cylinders or slabs to the orb so it rolls down the rails, then drop it into the floor switch to open the bars.

shrines.name: Sonapan Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, on the eastern cliff of Satori Mountain, southwest of Lookout Landing (approx -1921, 0357, 0228).
shrines.oneLine: Missing Pathways. Use Ascend on overhangs and slide the stone bricks to build paths across the gaps, repositioning blocks to reach each higher ledge.

shrines.name: Taki-ihaban Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, inside Lindor's Brow Cave just east of Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower (approx -1829, 1149, 0147).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; the cave is the trial. Gloom Hands lurk at the platform base, so sprint past and climb up to claim the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Usazum Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, just south of Satori Mountain Foothill Cave at the foot of Satori Mountain (approx -2139, -0874, 0093).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Activate the shrine; its green beam points to the cave. Take the crystal from the Hinox inside, then carry it back to the shrine to claim the blessing.

shrineRegions.notes: VERIFIED. Count and names confirmed correct: Hyrule Ridge (Surface) has exactly 5 shrines - Makurukis, Runakit, Sonapan, Taki-ihaban, Usazum - cross-checked against Nintendo Life (lists all 5 under \"Hyrule Ridge (5 Shrines)\") and Game8/gamerant/dotesports region lists. No invented or missing shrine names. CORRECTIONS MADE: (1) Sonapan oneLine was WRONG/INVENTED - it described fusing a metal cube onto a stone cube and Ascending through its exposed half. That mechanic does not exist in this shrine. The real \"Missing Pathways\" puzzle is moving/sliding stone bricks and using Ascend to create paths (per Shacknews, Game8, GameSkinny). Rewrote it. Also fixed Sonapan Y-coordinate from -0357 to +0357 (positive). (2) Runakit oneLine said \"build a cart\" - inaccurate. The real \"Built to Carry\" solution attaches stone cylinders/slabs to the orb so it rolls down rails into a floor switch (no cart is built). Rewrote to match. (3) Usazum oneLine had the beam direction BACKWARDS - it said follow the crystal's beam TO the shrine. Actually you activate the shrine first and ITS green beam points to the cave; you fight the Hinox, grab the crystal, and carry it BACK to the shrine. Rewrote. VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Makurukis (Combat Training: Archery; grab bow, headshot constructs, Ascend a side pillar for the trio - all confirmed); Taki-ihaban (Rauru's Blessing, no puzzle, inside Lindor's Brow Cave, Gloom Hands present - confirmed, refined coords to -1829,1149,0147 and \"east\" of the tower per guides); Usazum quest name \"The Satori Mountain Crystal\" and Hinox in Satori Mountain Foothill Cave - confirmed. Categories all sound per house rules. Taunhiy Shrine correctly EXCLUDED - it is the Hyrule Ridge SKY shrine, not Surface; if the app rolls Sky shrines under this regionKey, add Taunhiy (Combat Training: Archery, approx -2401,0825,0615). Region-boundary caveat: a few guides float Usazum toward eastern Hyrule Field and other guides inflate the region by pulling in Kiuyoyou (Great Hyrule Forest) or Tenmaten (Hyrule Field); Nintendo Life and Game8 both keep Usazum in Hyrule Ridge, so the 5-shrine Surface list stands. Confidence raised to 0.90 after multi-source verification.

Adversarially verified against Nintendo Life (15 Eldin shrines: 14 Surface + 1 Sky), cross-checked Game8, PowerPyx, Gamerant, segmentnext, Zelda Dungeon. All 15 names confirmed correct and belonging to Eldin. Kikakin (which one source mis-filed here) is correctly EXCLUDED - it's Great Hyrule Forest. Two fixes made: (1) Sitsum subtitle was WRONG - it is 'A Controlling Device' (steering-stick/vehicle-over-lava puzzle), NOT 'A Balanced Plan' (that's Rotsumamu in Gerudo); hint rewritten. (2) Sibajitak hint refined to its true rotating-pillar Recall+Ascend solution (subtitle 'Alignment' verified correct). Verified subtitles: Isisim 'In Reverse', Kimayat 'Smash', Mayachideg 'The Hunt' (all Proving Grounds/combat). Verified shrine-quest names: Momosik 'The Death Caldera Crystal', Moshapin 'The Lake Intenoch Cave Crystal'. Jiotak & Minetak are cave Blessing shrines with no puzzle. Kadaunar is the lone Eldin Sky shrine. No Lightroots included (those are Depths). Coordinates are approximate.

shrines.name: Sikukuu Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, southeast of Thyphlo Ruins (0696, 2792, 0225)
shrines.oneLine: Spinning Gears: use Recall to reverse the big gears so platforms carry you upward, riding them to the exit.

shrines.name: Marakuguc Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, on a cliff above Goron City (1761, 2508, 0437)
shrines.oneLine: Wheeled Wonders: Ultrahand the two wheeled carts together, ride your build, and strike the wheels to roll across the lava.

shrines.name: Timawak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, overlooking Bedrock Bistro (1798, 1635, 0311)
shrines.oneLine: Against the Flow: cross cooled-lava platforms, then Ultrahand spare platforms into a bridge to reach the orb and chest.

shrines.name: Isisim Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside YunoboCo HQ East Cave (1841, 2841, 0363)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: In Reverse. Use only provided gear; Recall the gears to flip enemy approaches and clear the Constructs.

shrines.name: Sitsum Shrine
shrines.location: Death Mountain, west side near the summit (2367, 2597, 0790)
shrines.oneLine: A Controlling Device: take a steering stick, drive a cart across the lava, then attach it to a fan-wing and fly to the exit.

shrines.name: Sibajitak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, north of Death Caldera (2399, 3269, 0402)
shrines.oneLine: Alignment: use Recall to line up the rotating pillar's segments, then Ascend straight up through them to reach the exit.

shrines.name: Kimayat Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, west of Skull Lake (2871, 3625, 0239)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Smash. Defeat all the Constructs (melee on the floor, archers above) with only the weapons provided.

shrines.name: Mayachideg Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, west of Kanalet Ridge (3062, 1817, 0216)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: The Hunt. Use only the items inside (start with a Wooden Stick) to hunt down and defeat the Constructs.

shrines.name: Mayak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, west of East Deplian Badlands (1270, 3733, 0106)
shrines.oneLine: A timing puzzle: launch up, Ultrahand a boulder onto the ramp, then swing the glowing post so it lands on the target.

shrines.name: Minetak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, inside Deplian Badlands Cave (0394, 3485, 0068)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: reach the shrine deep in the cave, open the chest, and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jiotak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside Isle of Rabac Gallery cave (1833, 3179, 0257)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: wear Flamebreaker gear, navigate the hot cave to the shrine, grab the chest, and claim the blessing.

shrines.name: Momosik Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, near Death Caldera (2957, 2759, 0524)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: appears after the crystal quest; enter, open the chest for a Big Battery, and take the blessing.

shrines.name: Moshapin Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside Lake Intenoch Cave (2678, 1905, 0131)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: carry the green crystal to the shrine to unlock it, open the chest, then claim the blessing.

shrines.name: Kisinona Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, between Maw of Death Mountain and Cephla Lake (2568, 1245, 0173)
shrines.oneLine: Wind Power: attach and activate the Zonai fans onto the turbine to generate wind and push platforms to the exit.

shrines.name: Kadaunar Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Sky, South Eldin Sky Archipelago (1882, 1202, 1251)
shrines.oneLine: Water Makes a Way: hit and carry the fire hydrant so its water cools lava into platforms, then fuse them into a bridge.

shrineRegions.notes: Web-verified against Nintendo Life (full Akkala map list), Game8, Gamerant, and gamepressure. Confirmed roster: 13 Akkala shrines = 10 Surface + 3 Sky, with these exact 13 names. The three Sky shrines are Natak and Gikaku (Akkala Highlands Sky / Akkala Sea Sky) plus Mogisari (Akkala Sea Sky, inside Lomei Sky Labyrinth). Boundary corrections: BOTH Mayachideg AND Kimayat shrines are Eldin Canyon, NOT Akkala, so both are excluded (gamepressure wrongly absorbed both into Akkala while dropping the three Sky shrines and Jochi-iu, yielding a bad 11-shrine list). GGRecon's 10-shrine list was incomplete. Category calls verified: Rasitakiwak is Proving Grounds: Vehicles (build a Zonai vehicle, no gear) and Sinatanika is Combat Training: Sneakstrike (stealth-strike tutorial) - both filed combat per rubric (Proving Grounds + combat trials map to combat) though both are gentle. The four blessing shrines (Jochi-ihiga, Igashuk, Natak, Gikaku) are Rauru's Blessing rooms; three are gated by quests (Jochi-ihiga = Rock for Sale, Natak = The Sokkala Sky Crystal, Gikaku = The Sky Mine Crystal), Igashuk is reached via the Lomei Labyrinth Island maze (no quest). Domizuin puzzle name confirmed as A Prone Pathway (rotation-pillar cube). Coordinates verified with minor digit variance between sources. Confidence 0.92: roster, count, and categories all solid; residual risk only on exact coordinate digits.

shrines.name: Domizuin Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, southeast of South Akkala Stable up the mountain (3305, 1443, 0426)
shrines.oneLine: A Prone Pathway: Hit the inside and outside rotation pillars to roll the giant cube until its steps line up, then platform to the exit.

shrines.name: Gatanisis Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, far east near Ulria Grotto by the Akkala Sea coast (4501, 0826, 0095)
shrines.oneLine: A Well-Timed Bounce: Recall the spring platform as the ball rolls across it to fling the ball up into the switch.

shrines.name: Jochi-iu Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, just northeast of East Akkala Stable (4350, 2972, 0164)
shrines.oneLine: Courage to Pluck: Ultrahand metal blocks out of the Jenga-like tower without toppling it, then climb to the altar.

shrines.name: Gemimik Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea, southeast of East Akkala Stable on the spiral Rist Peninsula island (4521, 2126, 0001)
shrines.oneLine: Turbine Power: Attach the propeller to the motor and bridge the wiring with a metal plate to power the fan and braziers.

shrines.name: Rasiwak Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, northeast of Akkala Ancient Tech Lab (4663, 3263, 0002)
shrines.oneLine: Flotational Brilliance: Fuse spheres and fans to metal planks so they float, then ferry a boat onto the exit switch.

shrines.name: Kamatukis Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, north of Tempest Gulch (3427, 3345, 0070)
shrines.oneLine: A Precise Strike: Aim launched balls and rotating cogs so a ball strikes the target switch to open the way forward.

shrines.name: Mogisari Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea Sky, inside the Lomei Sky Labyrinth (4655, 3496, 1010)
shrines.oneLine: Courage to Jump: Drive the makeshift carts and use the low gravity to leap the gaps; hug the left wall past the lasers.

shrines.name: Sinatanika Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, northeast of Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower (3842, 2299, 0048)
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training Sneakstrike: Stay crouched in the patrolling construct's blind spot and slip behind for a one-hit sneakstrike.

shrines.name: Rasitakiwak Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, southeast of Tarrey Town near Kaepora Pass (4161, 1324, 0229)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds Vehicles: Stripped of gear, fuse the emitters and cannon to a Zonai vehicle and ride it to crush the constructs.

shrines.name: Jochi-ihiga Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, northeast of Akkala Falls near Lake Akkala (3813, 1218, 0090)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Buy Hagie's green crystal in Tarrey Town and boat it to the light beam to reveal the shrine, then claim the reward.

shrines.name: Igashuk Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea, inside Lomei Labyrinth Island in the far northeast (4655, 3712, 0131)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Navigate the Lomei Labyrinth Island maze to reach the shrine, then walk to the altar for the free reward.

shrines.name: Natak Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands Sky, on the Sokkala Sky Archipelago (3673, 1486, 1157)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Rotate the model island with Ultrahand to align the real island, then launch the green crystal to the altar.

shrines.name: Gikaku Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea Sky, on the Sky Mine island (4505, 2166, 1155)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Complete The Sky Mine Crystal quest by delivering the green crystal up on Sky Mine to reveal the shrine.

shrineRegions.notes: 18 shrines total: 14 Surface + 4 Sky. Sub-regions: Lanayru Wetlands, Lanayru Great Spring (Zora's Domain), Mount Lanayru, and Lanayru Great Spring Sky. Web-verified across Game8, gamepressure, SegmentNext, Zelda Dungeon, Fextralife, GameRant, PowerPyx, Thonky. All 18 names are real TotK shrines. Count caveat confirmed: SegmentNext lists only 17 (omits Morok); adding Morok gives 18. Corrections made: (1) Morok relocated to near Sahasra Slope (NW of Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower), not SE of Wetland Stable. (2) Mayanas shrineQuest corrected to 'The South Lanayru Sky Crystal' (the materializing quest); 'The Ice Guides You' is the internal puzzle theme, and the puzzle is an ice/Frost Emitter water-freezing puzzle, not a hoverboard/beam (that prior hint was invented). (3) Sihajog oneLine corrected: it is unlocked by the Valor Island Dive Ceremony skydive (pass through green rings), not just by reaching it. (4) Kurakat oneLine corrected: 'Dyeing to Find It' is a dyeing riddle (dye your armor the riddle's color), not a crystal-carry. (5) Ihen-a oneLine tightened to the real Hover Stone + bridge/ramp mechanic. (6) Jonsau location cleaned (it sits on Mercay Island in the central Wetlands; the 'Wetlands Chasm' reference was inaccurate). The three quest-gated reward shrines (Joniu, Kurakat, O-ogim) plus Jogou, Yomizuk, Maoikes, and Sihajog are blessings; Zakusu is the lone Proving Grounds (combat). All hints under 170 chars.

shrines.name: Tukarok Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, just south of Wetland Stable
shrines.oneLine: Forward Force. Stick the ball to a wheeled cart and ride it across lava, then build a paddle raft to ferry the ball to the altar.

shrines.name: Morok Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, atop a floating island near Sahasra Slope, northwest of Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: A Bouncy Device. Stack and fuse the Zonai springs, then activate them to launch yourself up to the chest and the altar.

shrines.name: Jonsau Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, on the southern hills of Mercay Island near the center of the Wetlands
shrines.oneLine: Deep Force. Ultrahand the ball under the target and push it deep underwater so it rockets up and strikes the switch.

shrines.name: Maoikes Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, inside Bone Pond Cave (drop in through the skull on the hilltop)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; just reach it through the cave, grab the chest, and touch the altar.

shrines.name: Mogawak Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, below the walkways of Zora's Domain beneath the Great Zora Bridge
shrines.oneLine: The Power of Water. Charge the battery with the waterfall's hydro power to run the elevator up to the altar.

shrines.name: Ihen-a Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, Mipha Court atop Ploymus Mountain, east of Zora's Domain
shrines.oneLine: Midair Perch. Splash-arrow the gooped entrance, then Ascend up a Hover Stone and lay the bridge as a ramp to cross the gaps.

shrines.name: Apogek Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, southeast of East Reservoir Lake near Zora's Domain
shrines.oneLine: Wings on the Wind. Fuse a fan to a Zonai wing, set it on the rail, then ride it across the room to the altar.

shrines.name: Yomizuk Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, inside Tarm Point Cave, north of Tarm Point
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle; find it through the cave, open the chest, and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joniu Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, inside Ralis Channel, northwest of Veiled Falls
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing, unlocked via a shrine quest. Toss a Brightbloom Seed to light the dark channel and follow the right path in.

shrines.name: Kurakat Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, northwest of Quatta's Shelf
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing behind a dyeing riddle: read the clue, dye your armor the right color at Hateno, then claim the chest and altar.

shrines.name: O-ogim Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, south of Lanayru Heights below the broken Lanayru Bridge
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing reward for a shrine quest; deliver the crystal to make the shrine appear, then take the chest and altar.

shrines.name: Jikais Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, east of the Lanayru Range beyond Madorna Mountain
shrines.oneLine: Jailbreak. Use Ultrahand to stack and move blocks, then Ascend up through them to reach the altar.

shrines.name: Zakusu Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, Naydra Snowfield, southwest of Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Ascension. Stripped of gear, use only the shrine's weapons and Ascend to beat the foes; gated behind a shrine quest.
shrines.shrineQuest: The High Spring and the Light Rings

shrines.name: Jogou Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, inside Lanayru Road East Cave on the northwest slopes (below the Lanayru Road East Gate)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; just find it within the cave and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jirutagumac Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring Sky, inside a floating orb-shaped island west of Wellspring Island
shrines.oneLine: A Flying Device. Find the floating orb island, get inside it, and examine the shrine within to clear it.

shrines.name: Igoshon Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring Sky, on a high ledge by a large waterfall on Wellspring Island
shrines.oneLine: Orbs of Water. When water orbs fire toward the altar, dive inside one and ride it across to the far side.

shrines.name: Mayanas Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring Sky, northern edge of Valor Island (reach via Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower)
shrines.oneLine: The Ice Guides You. Fire Frost Emitters to freeze the water into ice sheets and build a path of ice across to the altar.

shrines.name: Sihajog Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring Sky, upper area of Valor Island near the snowman structure
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Unlocked by the Valor Island Dive Ceremony: skydive through all the green rings, then touch the altar.

shrineRegions.notes: VERIFIED against Nintendo Life's regional grouping (the cited source), cross-checked with Game8, Gamerant, and segmentnext. Nintendo Life lists exactly 18 Necluda shrines and the names match this object one-for-one: Sky (4) = Kumamayn, Josiu, Ukoojisi, Yansamin; Surface (14) = Makasura, Joju-u-u, Eshos, Sifumim, Bamitok, Marari-In, Zanmik, Mayahisik, Anedamimik, Utojis, Jochisiu, Jogou, Susub, Tokiy. CORRECTION: changed "Sifumin" to the canonical in-game spelling "Sifumim" (used by Game8/Gamerant). Disputed border shrines resolved: Jiukoum is confirmed FARON (north of Spring of Courage / Popla Foothills), NOT Necluda - correctly excluded (uncertainty in prior notes is now resolved). Morok is confirmed Lanayru Wetlands (near Sahasra Slope), correctly excluded. Jikais, Zakusu, Kurakat, O-ogim are Lanayru, correctly excluded. Category calls verified: Eshos = Combat Training: Shields (combat); Sifumim = Proving Grounds: Flow, knock Constructs into water (combat); Yansamin = Proving Grounds: Low Gravity, defeat 9 Constructs, needs Zora Armor (combat); Tokiy = Rauru's Blessing revealed by The Oakle's Navel Cave Crystal quest (blessing); Susub/Mayahisik/Bamitok/Jogou = Rauru's Blessing (blessing); rest = puzzle. Jogou confirmed in East Necluda (Lanayru Road East Cave, Rauru's Blessing). Anedamimik "A Retraced Path" Recall-the-ball solution verified. No invented shrines found; count and membership are correct. Remaining minor uncertainty: a few guides draw the Necluda/Lanayru border slightly differently, but this matches the cited Nintendo Life partition.

shrines.name: Makasura Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, on a cliff just west/southwest above Kakariko Village; NE of Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Use Ascend through the rock ceilings and ride the stone platforms up; cross the gaps to reach the exit.

shrines.name: Joju-u-u Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, Ubota Point south of Lakeside Stable, above the cliffs by Lake Floria.
shrines.oneLine: Building Bridges: use Ultrahand to drape the hinged bridge over the post and add the stone box as a counterweight.

shrines.name: Jochisiu Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, west side of South Dueling Peaks by Squabble River.
shrines.oneLine: Keys Born of Water quest: use the fire and ice emitters to melt/freeze ice and clear the path, then enter.

shrines.name: Eshos Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, on the eastern cliff of Dueling Peaks.
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Shields. Parry the Training Constructs' attacks; use a non-metallic shield against the electric one.

shrines.name: Susub Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, inside Deya Village Ruins East Well, south of Deya Village Ruins.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Drop into the well and reach the shrine; grab the chest and the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Utojis Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, inside Tobio's Hollow Cave.
shrines.oneLine: Legend of the Soaring Spear: fuse a Keese Wing to a spear, stand on the glowing pedestal and throw it through the ring.

shrines.name: Tokiy Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Oakle's Navel Cave (north of Rabella Wetlands Skyview Tower).
shrines.oneLine: The Oakle's Navel Cave Crystal: carry the crystal past falling boulders (Recall the big one) to its stand to reveal it.

shrines.name: Zanmik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, on a hill southwest of Hateno Village.
shrines.oneLine: Scoop it Out: Ultrahand four plates into a box, attach it to the wheel as a scoop, then plate the nodes to power it.

shrines.name: Mayahisik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Retsam Forest Cave near the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Enter the cave, break the rocks and follow the path to the shrine for the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Anedamimik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Deepback Bay Cave, east of Hateno Village.
shrines.oneLine: A Retraced Path: ride the moving platform across, then use Recall on the rolling ball to send it into the receptacle.

shrines.name: Bamitok Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Mount Dunsel Cave near Lurelin Village.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Swim across, lift the plank and Ascend up to reach the shrine and Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jogou Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Lanayru Road East Cave.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle inside, but the cave path is tough. Reach the shrine and claim the Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sifumim Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, on a hill just northwest of Lurelin Village.
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Flow. Weapons removed. Knock the Soldier Constructs off the rotating platforms into the water.

shrines.name: Marari-In Shrine
shrines.location: Necluda Sea, on Eventide Island via the sea cave entrance (far southeast).
shrines.oneLine: Solve the puzzle inside the Eventide cave to reveal it; reach it by raft/boat or paraglide from the Hateno coast.

shrines.name: Josiu Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda Sky, on a sky island east of the Great Sky Island area above Necluda.
shrines.oneLine: North Necluda Sky Crystal: Ultrahand the rotating arm, Recall it to ferry the green crystal back to the stand.

shrines.name: Ukoojisi Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda Sky, on a sky island directly above South Dueling Peaks.
shrines.oneLine: Launch from Popla Foothills Skyview Tower and paraglide to the island; manage stamina to land on the shrine platform.

shrines.name: Kumamayn Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda Sky, on a plus-shaped island east of Rabella Wetlands Skyview Tower.
shrines.oneLine: The Necluda Sky Crystal: follow the launcher islands to the cross-shaped island and carry the crystal to its stand.

shrines.name: Yansamin Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda Sky, on Zonaite Forge Island above Ash Swamp (needs Zora Armor to climb the waterfall).
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Low Gravity. Weapons removed; use the low gravity and Fuse parts to defeat the nine Constructs.

shrines.name: En-oma Shrine
shrines.location: Lake Hylia Whirlpool Cave, beneath Lake Hylia (approx 0105, -2514, 0088); enter by letting the visible whirlpool suck Link down
shrines.oneLine: No trial inside; reward for The Lake Hylia Crystal quest. Grab the crystal on the sky island above the whirlpool and drop it into the hole below.

shrines.name: Ishokin Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands near Zokassa Ridge, east of Oseira Plains (approx -0565, -3524, 0129)
shrines.oneLine: No trial inside; appears after Ride the Giant Horse. Tame the Giant White Stallion in the nearby grove and show it to Baddek for the crystal.

shrines.name: Jiukoum Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands, south side of Popla Foothills overlooking Dracozu Lake, SE of Popla Foothills Skyview Tower (approx 0867, -2279, 0141)
shrines.oneLine: Built for Rails: use Ultrahand to join the boards into a stable platform, set it on the rails, and ride it across each gap to the altar.

shrines.name: Utsushok Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands, northeast of Highland Stable, east of Fural Plain atop a hill (approx 0668, -3358, 0072)
shrines.oneLine: Long or Wide: with Ultrahand add length or weight to the paddle to knock balls into switches, then ride the spawned cart along the rails.

shrines.name: Joku-u Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Sky, Dragonhead Island (Thunderhead Isles) (approx 1375, -3339, 0429), right by the door to Mineru's Mask room
shrines.oneLine: No trial inside; the storm-shrouded trek is the challenge. Clear the storm via the Fifth Sage questline, then enter for the chest and Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joku-usin Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Sky, southern part of Dragonhead Island (Thunderhead Isles); revealed via the Find the Fifth Sage main quest (approx 1070, -3342, 0785)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Short Circuit. Stripped of gear, beat the Constructs and a Thunder Wizzrobe; Fuse shock weapons or use water to chain electric damage.

shrineRegions.notes: CORRECTED to 6 Faron shrines: 4 Surface (En-oma, Ishokin, Jiukoum, Utsushok) + 2 Faron Sky (Joku-u, Joku-usin). Matches Nintendo Life's authoritative Faron map. KEY FIX: removed Joju-u-u Shrine from the original 7 - it is NOT Faron. Joju-u-u sits on Ubota Point in WEST NECLUDA overlooking Lakeside Stable (confirmed by gamepressure's Necluda page, Zelda Dungeon, GameRant); it is a puzzle (Building Bridges) but belongs to Necluda. This resolves the prior 5-vs-7 boundary doubt. Also confirmed Susub (West Necluda, Deya Village Ruins) and Utojis (East Necluda, Tobio's Hollow) are correctly excluded - GGRecon wrongly bundled all three (Joju-u-u, Susub, Utojis) into a 9-shrine Faron list. FIXES TO HINTS: Joku-usin access was wrong in original (said 'after clearing the four Regional Phenomena'); it is actually revealed via the Find the Fifth Sage main quest on Dragonhead Island/Thunderhead Isles - corrected. Joku-u storm note refined (clears via the Ring Ruins/Fifth Sage questline). Categories verified: En-oma/Ishokin/Joku-u = Rauru's Blessing; Jiukoum/Utsushok = puzzle; Joku-usin = combat (Proving Grounds: Short Circuit, vs Soldier Constructs + Thunder Wizzrobe). Ishokin note: you may tame the Giant Horse OR Giant White Stallion - kept Stallion as the recommended/closer option. Coordinates approximate (minor source variance). Confidence 0.9; only residual uncertainty is coordinate precision.

shrines.name: En-oma Shrine
shrines.location: Lake Hylia Whirlpool Cave, reached by diving into the whirlpool in the center of Lake Hylia (approx. 0104, -2517, -0087)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing, no puzzle. From The Lake Hylia Crystal quest: grab the crystal on the sky island above, drop it down the whirlpool, set it at the shrine.

shrines.name: Jiukoum Shrine
shrines.location: On a rise overlooking Dracozu Lake, southeast of Popla Foothills Skyview Tower, north edge of Faron Grasslands (approx. 0869, -2279, 0141)
shrines.oneLine: Built for Rails: Ultrahand metal sheets into shapes that ride sliding rails across gaps, then add Zonai fans to power your creation along the final track.

shrines.name: Utojis Shrine
shrines.location: Inside Tobio's Hollow Cave, east of Lake Hylia near the East Necluda border (approx. 1219, -2545, 0096)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Unlocked by the Legend of the Soaring Spear quest: fuse a Keese Wing onto a Zonaite Spear and throw it through the floating ring.

shrineRegions.notes:
VERIFICATION RESULT: All three listed shrines are REAL TotK content and now accurately described; no invented shrines found. Cross-checked across PowerPyx, Game8, GameRant, GameSpot, GGRecon, and Zelda Dungeon/Zelda Wiki.

CORRECTIONS MADE: (1) En-oma oneLine simplified; it is firmly a Rauru's Blessing tied to The Lake Hylia Crystal quest (correct). Note z-coord varies by source (-0087 vs +0088); the negative is the in-cave value. (2) Jiukoum quest name is officially \"Built for Rails\" (was paraphrased \"Trial Built for Rails\"); puzzle is Ultrahand metal sheets + rails + fans (correct). It overlooks Dracozu Lake in Faron Grasslands, NOT a separate \"Lake Hylia\" sub-region. (3) Utojis: the original location wrongly implied Faron; multiple guides tag it EAST NECLUDA (Tobio's Hollow Cave, east of Lake Hylia). Quest is Legend of the Soaring Spear: fuse a KEESE WING (not generic wings) onto a Zonaite Spear and throw through the ring/portal (correct).

REGION CAVEAT (why confidence is not higher): TotK has no standalone official map region called \"Lake.\" Lake Hylia falls within the FARON region (Faron Tower / Popla Foothills). regionKey=\"lake\" is a BotW-style carryover, so the exact intended roster depends on the app's own region boundaries. Only En-oma is unambiguously AT Lake Hylia. Jiukoum and Utojis are the nearest lake-adjacent shrines and are reasonable inclusions for a broadly drawn Lake region; if the app instead routes the broader Faron grouping elsewhere, these two may belong under \"faron.\"

EXCLUDED (correctly not in this list): Ishokin (-0562,-3524, SW of Lake Hylia), Utsushok (0668,-3358), Joju-u-u (1516,-3576), Susub (-0785,-0433), and the Faron sky shrines Joku-u (1372,-3336,0428) and Joku-usin (1070,-3342,0785) all sit well into Faron Grasslands/sky and away from Lake Hylia proper.

BAD-DATA WARNINGS CONFIRMED: Mayaotaki Shrine is in the North Lomei Labyrinth (Hebra), NOT Lake Hylia's west shore. Ukouh is the Great Sky Island tutorial shrine, NOT a Lake shrine. Neither belongs here.

Recommend the team confirm against the app's own region-boundary definition before finalizing.

abilities.name: Ultrahand
abilities.what: Grab, move, rotate and glue almost any object together to build bridges, vehicles, and machines from Zonai parts.
abilities.from: Ukouh Shrine, your first shrine on the Great Sky Island.
abilities.tip: Hold the button to fine-tune angle and distance; objects snap and turn yellow when they'll stick. Press up on the D-pad to detach mistakes.

abilities.name: Fuse
abilities.what: Attach an object or material to a weapon, shield, or arrow to boost damage, change its effect, or add reach.
abilities.from: In-isa Shrine, the second Great Sky Island shrine.
abilities.tip: Fuse a rock onto a flimsy stick for a hammer, or a Keese eyeball onto arrows for homing shots. It saves your weak weapons from breaking fast.

abilities.name: Ascend
abilities.what: Swim straight up through any ceiling above you and pop out standing on top of it.
abilities.from: Gutanbac Shrine, the third Great Sky Island shrine.
abilities.tip: Use it to skip climbing in caves and towers. Aim for a flat ceiling overhead, not slopes, and watch the cursor turn yellow when it's valid.

abilities.name: Recall
abilities.what: Reverse an object's recent movement, sending it back along the exact path it just traveled.
abilities.from: From Zelda's spirit at the Temple of Time on the Great Sky Island, after clearing the first three shrines.
abilities.tip: Ride a fallen sky rock back up to the islands, or send enemy projectiles flying back. Great for reaching high places without building.

abilities.name: Autobuild
abilities.what: Instantly recreate saved or recently built Zonai constructions, spending Zonaite for parts you don't have on hand.
abilities.from: From a Construct at the Great Abandoned Central Mine in the Depths, during the quest A Mystery in the Depths (not on the Great Sky Island).
abilities.tip: Save favorite vehicles like a hover bike so you can rebuild them anywhere. Capturing schematics from machines you find expands your options.

abilities.notes: Verified all five against multiple TotK walkthroughs. CORRECTIONS MADE: (1) Autobuild's source was invented/wrong — the original claimed "Spirit Temple region during Mineru's questline." That conflates Mineru (the fifth sage, Spirit Temple) with Autobuild. Autobuild is actually granted by a Construct at the Great Abandoned Central Mine in the Depths during the main quest "A Mystery in the Depths," followed by the Master Kohga boss fight — no Mineru/Spirit Temple involvement. (2) Recall "from" reworded to credit Zelda's spirit (an ethereal Princess Zelda at the Temple of Time pedestal), confirmed by sources. (3) Ultrahand tip: glued joints highlight yellow, not green — fixed to "turn yellow." (4) Trimmed unverifiable precise compass directions ("southwest of Ukouh," "east side") from Fuse/Ascend "from" fields; the first three shrine order (Ukouh, In-isa, Gutanbac) is correct. CONFIRMED CORRECT: Ultrahand=Ukouh, Fuse=In-isa, Ascend=Gutanbac, Recall=Temple of Time after the first three shrines, and that Nachoyah is the fourth Great Sky Island shrine — a blessing/test shrine that uses Recall but grants no new ability. High confidence. Sources: nintendolife.com Great Sky Island walkthrough, game8.co Nachoyah/Recall/Autobuild guides, gamingbolt.com all-abilities guide, gamewith.net Autobuild guide, zeldadungeon.net, segmentnext.com Recall guide.

sets.name: Archaic / Well-Worn
sets.pieces: Archaic Tunic, Archaic Legwear, Well-Worn Trousers (no headpiece)
sets.where: Starting gear on the Great Sky Island; from chests and the Steward Construct as you begin the game.
sets.bonus: No set bonus; just basic starter defense to get you off the tutorial island.
sets.upgrade: Not upgradable at Great Fairies; starter clothing you replace fast.

sets.name: Hylian Set
sets.pieces: Hylian Hood, Hylian Tunic, Hylian Trousers
sets.where: Buy from Hateno Village's Ventest Clothing Boutique and other early shops.
sets.bonus: No set bonus, but cheap, balanced all-purpose defense for the early game.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: common monster parts like Bokoblin Horns plus Hylian plants.

sets.name: Climbing Gear (Climber's Set)
sets.pieces: Climber's Bandanna, Climbing Gear (chest), Climbing Boots
sets.where: Three separate chests in caves: North Hyrule Plain Cave, Ploymus Mountain Cave, and Upland Zorana Byroad.
sets.bonus: Full set bonus Climbing Jump Stamina Up: halves the stamina used when jumping mid-climb. No per-piece speed boost.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: lizards and climbing critters like Lizalfos Tails.

sets.name: Glide Set
sets.pieces: Glide Mask, Glide Shirt, Glide Tights
sets.where: Complete the three Sky dive ring challenges on Courage, Bravery, and Valor Islands (one piece each).
sets.bonus: Each piece boosts dive speed; full set upgraded to 2 stars gives Impact Proof - immunity to all fall damage.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: sky/flight critters and Zonai materials.
sets.priority: Fun and very useful for fliers

sets.name: Zonaite Set
sets.pieces: Zonaite Helm, Zonaite Waistguard, Zonaite Greaves
sets.where: Three chests on the Sky Islands, in different areas of the sky.
sets.bonus: Full set bonus Energy Recharge Up: doubles how fast your Zonai battery (energy cells) recharge.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: Zonaite ore and Zonai device/construct materials.

sets.name: Charged Set
sets.pieces: Charged Headdress, Charged Shirt, Charged Trousers
sets.where: Three caves along the Dracozu River (marked by Tall Dragon Pillars) during the Secret of the Ring Ruins quest.
sets.bonus: Full set bonus Stormy Weather Charge: charges weapon attacks faster while in stormy weather.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: electric monster parts (Electric Lizalfos, Electric Keese).

sets.name: Froggy Set
sets.pieces: Froggy Hood, Froggy Sleeve, Froggy Leggings
sets.where: Rewards from completing all of Penn's Lucky Clover Gazette side quests (12 stable jobs) for Traysi.
sets.bonus: Slip Resistance per piece; full set greatly reduces slipping while climbing wet or icy surfaces.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: frogs like Hot-Footed Frog and Tireless Frog.

sets.name: Rubber Set
sets.pieces: Rubber Helm, Rubber Armor, Rubber Tights
sets.where: Three chests in caves around Hyrule; the helm is an easy early grab before facing Gerudo storms.
sets.bonus: Shock Resistance per piece; fully upgraded set bonus Unshockable negates electric/lightning shock damage.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: electric parts like Electric Lizalfos Tails.

sets.name: Desert Voe Set
sets.pieces: Desert Voe Headband, Desert Voe Spaulder, Desert Voe Trousers
sets.where: Buy from the clothing shop in Gerudo Town once you reach the desert.
sets.bonus: Heat Resistance per piece; fully upgraded set bonus adds Shock Damage Resist, ideal for the Gerudo region.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: desert critters and Electric Safflina.

sets.name: Snowquill Set
sets.pieces: Snowquill Headdress, Snowquill Tunic, Snowquill Trousers
sets.where: Buy from the Rito Village shop (Brazen Beak) in the snowy Hebra/Tabantha region.
sets.bonus: Cold Resistance per piece; fully upgraded set bonus Unfreezable keeps you from freezing in blizzards.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: cold-region critters (Cold Darner, Winterwing Butterfly).

sets.name: Flamebreaker Set
sets.pieces: Flamebreaker Helm, Flamebreaker Armor, Flamebreaker Boots
sets.where: Buy at the Goron armor shop in Goron City near Death Mountain.
sets.bonus: Fire Resistance per piece; fully upgraded set bonus Fireproof stops flames from burning you.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: fire materials like Fireproof Lizards and Flint.

sets.name: Zora Set (Zora Armor)
sets.pieces: Zora Helm, Zora Armor, Zora Greaves
sets.where: Zora Armor from Lady Yona during Sidon of the Zora (needs an ancient arowana; Dento points you to it). Helm and greaves found around the region.
sets.bonus: Swim Speed Up per piece; full set lets you swim up waterfalls, very handy in Zora areas.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: fish and Zora-region materials.

sets.name: Champion's Leathers
sets.where: Hidden chest in the Sanctum (throne room) of Hyrule Castle; light the two torches behind the throne to open the tomb.
sets.bonus: No set bonus (single piece), but strong scaling defense as you upgrade and a classic blue look.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: Star Fragments plus rarer monster parts at higher stars.

sets.name: Miner's Set (glow set)
sets.pieces: Miner's Mask, Miner's Top, Miner's Trousers
sets.where: Three chests in Depths mines: Abandoned Kara Kara, Daphnes Canyon, and Hylia Canyon mines.
sets.bonus: Pieces glow to light the dark Depths; full upgraded set bonus Shining Steps leaves a glowing petal trail.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: Brightcaps and luminous Depths materials.
sets.priority: Best early light source for the Depths

sets.name: Tunic of the Wild (Of the Wild Set)
sets.pieces: Cap of the Wild, Tunic of the Wild, Trousers of the Wild
sets.where: Misko's Treasure side quests - three Dark Skeletons in the Depths (Hebra, Gerudo, Eldin). The Eldin piece needs fire protection. NOT an all-shrines reward like BotW.
sets.bonus: Full set upgraded to 2 stars grants Attack Up. The classic green Champion look.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: Dragon Parts (scales, claws, horns, fangs).

sets.name: Tunic of Memories
sets.pieces: Single body piece (not part of a set)
sets.where: Buy for 400 Poes from any Bargainer Statue after speaking to all of them and clearing A Call from the Depths.
sets.bonus: No set bonus; a nostalgic outfit referencing Link's classic green tunic, mainly for the look.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: Depths/Gloom-adjacent materials at the Great Fairies.

sets.name: Fierce Deity Set
sets.pieces: Fierce Deity Mask, Fierce Deity Armor, Fierce Deity Boots
sets.where: Three caves (Skull Lake Cave, Citadel Ruins Summit Cave, Ancient Tree Stump) without amiibo, or via the Majora's Mask Link amiibo.
sets.bonus: Attack Up per piece; full set upgraded to 3 stars adds the Charge Atk. Stamina Up bonus. Strong offensive set.
sets.upgrade: Upgrade theme: rare monster horns/guts at higher stars.

armor.notes:
Adversarial verify done via web research (Game8, Fextralife, Zelda Dungeon, Nintendo Life, DualShockers, Sportskeeda). Errors fixed below; all remaining facts confirmed against multiple sources.

ERRORS CORRECTED:
1) Climbing Gear: WRONG that pieces are shrine rewards with "Climbing Speed Up per piece" and a "climbing leap." Real: pieces are in three CAVE chests; the full-set bonus is Climbing Jump Stamina Up (halves stamina used when jumping mid-climb). No per-piece speed effect.
2) Charged Set: Raised from low confidence. Set bonus name confirmed = Stormy Weather Charge (charges attacks faster in storms), NOT a shock-resistance bonus. Pieces are in Dracozu River caves marked by Tall Dragon Pillars (Secret of the Ring Ruins).
3) Champion's Leathers: WRONG that Josha/Purah give it. Real: hidden chest in Hyrule Castle Sanctum (throne room) - light the two torches behind the throne to open the tomb.
4) Tunic of the Wild: WRONG set bonus. It is Attack Up, NOT "Master Sword Beam Up" (that bonus belongs to the separate Ancient Hero's Aspect). Source clarified as Misko's Treasure side quests (three Dark Skeletons in the Depths), confirming it is NOT the BotW all-shrines reward.
5) Zora Set: WRONG that Zora Armor comes "from Dento." Real: Lady Yona gives it during Sidon of the Zora after you fetch an ancient arowana; Dento only tells you where to find one.
6) Fierce Deity Set: WRONG that it is amiibo-only / "not findable in the world." Real: all three pieces are findable in caves (Skull Lake Cave, Citadel Ruins Summit Cave, Ancient Tree Stump); amiibo is an alternate route. Each piece gives Attack Up; full 3-star set bonus is Charge Atk. Stamina Up (not "Attack Up").
7) Flamebreaker Set: set-bonus name corrected to Fireproof (dataset said "Flame Guard").
8) Rubber Set: set-bonus name corrected to Unshockable (dataset said "Shockproof").
9) Froggy Set: softened "never slip" to accurate "greatly reduces slipping"; clarified source is all 12 of Penn's Lucky Clover Gazette stable jobs.

CONFIRMED CORRECT (unchanged facts): Archaic/Well-Worn starter gear; Hylian set from Ventest Clothing Boutique; Glide Set from 3 sky dive challenges with Impact Proof at 2 stars; Zonaite Energy Recharge Up; Snowquill Unfreezable; Desert Voe Heat Res + Shock Damage Resist when upgraded; Miner's Set mine locations + Shining Steps; Zora swim-up-waterfalls; Tunic of Memories 400 Poes after A Call from the Depths; Bargainer Statues total seven (1 Lookout Landing + 6 Depths).

REMAINING LOWER CONFIDENCE: exact in-game wording of per-piece "dive speed" effect names (Glide) and the precise cave coordinates for Rubber/Zora helm-greaves were kept general rather than fabricated.

REMINDER FOR THE APP: Resistance immunities (Unfreezable, Fireproof, Unshockable, Impact Proof) and most listed set bonuses unlock only after upgrading the full set to 2 stars at a Great Fairy, and Great Fairies must first be freed via the Stable Trotters questlines. Worth surfacing as a tip. Voice kept concise, warm, spoiler-aware; every step/bonus/where string kept plain text and under 170 chars.

enemies.name: Bokoblin
enemies.tactic: Sneak up for a stealth strike, or flurry-rush their swing. Fuse a horn or rock to your weapon for far higher damage on early ones.
enemies.drops: Bokoblin Horn, Bokoblin Fang, Bokoblin Guts

enemies.name: Moblin
enemies.tactic: Big and slow. Dodge the wide club swing for a flurry rush, or knock it off ledges. Fused horns/rocks make short work of it.
enemies.drops: Moblin Horn, Moblin Fang, Moblin Guts

enemies.name: Lizalfos
enemies.tactic: Fast and dodgy; they spit elemental bursts. Hit with the opposite element or just flurry-rush their lunge, then finish quickly.
enemies.drops: Lizalfos Horn, Lizalfos Tail, Lizalfos Talon

enemies.name: Horriblin
enemies.tactic: Clings to cave ceilings and grabs you with its tongue. Shoot the head to drop it, then beat it on the ground. Higher tiers spit gloom.
enemies.drops: Horriblin Horn, Horriblin Claw, Horriblin Guts

enemies.name: Aerocuda
enemies.tactic: Flying bat-eye that swoops and can carry off Bokoblins. Shoot it down with any arrow; a Keese Eyeball-fused arrow homes right in.
enemies.drops: Aerocuda Eyeball, Aerocuda Wing

enemies.name: Construct (Soldier / Captain)
enemies.tactic: Zonai automata in the Sky. Soldiers are basic; Captains hit harder. Flurry-rush, or knock their weapon off to steal a Construct weapon to Fuse.
enemies.drops: Soldier/Captain Construct Horn, Zonai Charge, Construct weapons

enemies.name: Like Like
enemies.tactic: Tube that swallows you and spits you out, dropping a weapon. Shoot the open mouth or its eye to stun, then attack. Watch for Ice/Rock variants.
enemies.drops: Opal, chest gear; Ice Like Stone or Rock Like Stone from variants

enemies.name: Gibdo
enemies.tactic: Gerudo desert mummies that harden and revive unless finished with an element. Hit with fire/electric/ice to weaken, then strike to end them.
enemies.drops: Gibdo Bone, Gibdo Wing, Gibdo Guts

enemies.name: Gloom Hands (Gloom Spawn)
enemies.tactic: Crawling gloom arms that drain max hearts. Get high ground and rain Bomb Flower arrows into the cluster; bright light (Brightbloom seeds) also weakens them.
enemies.drops: Dark Clump

enemies.name: Phantom Ganon
enemies.tactic: Spawns after clearing Gloom Hands. Wear gloom-resist gear; flurry-rush his lunges with a strong fast weapon, then punish. Repeat through his phases.
enemies.drops: Demon King's Bow, a Gloom weapon (Sword/Club/Spear), Dark Clump

enemies.name: Boss Bokoblin
enemies.tactic: Huge Bokoblin that leads camps and charges. Sidestep the rush for a flurry rush, hit the head, and use a heavy Fuse (Silver Bokoblin Horn, rocks).
enemies.drops: Boss Bokoblin Horn, Boss Bokoblin Guts

enemies.name: Hinox
enemies.tactic: One-eyed giant; sleeps with weapons stuck in it. Shoot the eye to stagger, then melee. Loot the weapons off its necklace before finishing.
enemies.drops: Hinox Horn, Hinox Guts, Hinox Toenail, Hinox Tooth

enemies.name: Stalnox
enemies.tactic: Skeletal Hinox in caves/Depths. Shoot the eye to make the head pop off, grab the loose head with Ultrahand or smash it before the body reassembles.
enemies.drops: Stalnox Horn, Hinox Tooth

enemies.name: Stone Talus (incl. Luminous / Rare)
enemies.tactic: Rock golem with an ore weak spot on its back. Climb up and hammer the black ore, or Ascend through it to reach the top. Luminous/Rare variants drop gems.
enemies.drops: Flint, Amber, Luminous Stone; gems like Topaz/Diamond/Ruby/Sapphire on rare types

enemies.name: Frox
enemies.tactic: Giant Depths frog-toad. Shoot its eye (Dazzle Fruit or Keese-eye arrow) to topple it, climb on and smash the glowing Zonaite ore on its back. Repeat.
enemies.drops: Frox Fang, Frox Fingernail, Frox Guts, Zonaite, Large Zonaite, Crystallized Charge

enemies.name: Flux Construct
enemies.tactic: Floating cube golem; hit or Ultrahand-pull the glowing block to break it apart. In ball form dodge its body-slam, then strike the weak block. I/II/III scale up.
enemies.drops: Flux Construct Core, Zonaite, Large Zonaite

enemies.name: Molduga
enemies.tactic: Sand shark in Gerudo dunes. Drop a Bomb Flower (or use a fused bomb) to lure it up, hit the bomb to flip it onto land, then wail on it while it's beached.
enemies.drops: Molduga Fin, Molduga Jaw, Molduga Guts

enemies.name: Gleeok (Fire / Frost / Thunder)
enemies.tactic: Multi-headed dragon. Shoot each head to stun, then in its air phase use an elemental-counter arrow (e.g. Frost for Fire) and finish heads fast.
enemies.drops: Fire/Frost/Thunder Gleeok Horn, Wing, Guts

enemies.name: King Gleeok
enemies.tactic: Toughest Gleeok, all three elements. Bring lots of arrows and a fast bow; stun all three heads, then in the aerial storm phase hit heads with bomb/elemental arrows.
enemies.drops: King Gleeok Horn, Wing, Guts

enemies.name: Lynel
enemies.tactic: Brutal centaur. Shoot the face to stun, mount it for free hits, and flurry-rush its charge. Fuse a Lynel horn for a devastating weapon. Save before trying.
enemies.drops: Lynel Saber Horn or Mace Horn, Lynel Hoof, Lynel Guts, Lynel weapons

enemies.name: Temple bosses (Colgera, Marbled Gohma, Mucktorok, Queen Gibdo)
enemies.tactic: Each temple boss is solved by its dungeon gimmick plus your sage's power: Colgera (Wind), Marbled Gohma (Fire), Mucktorok (Water), Queen Gibdo (Lightning).
enemies.drops: Heart Container, sage's vow

enemies.name: Demon King Ganondorf
enemies.tactic: Final humanoid fight; he mirrors your moves and inflicts gloom. Use gloom-resist food, flurry-rush his combos, and call all five sages to swarm him.

enemies.name: Demon Dragon
enemies.tactic: Final form, fought in free-fall. Dive onto its back and hit the four glowing weak spots with the Master Sword (10x dmg); the Light Dragon lifts you between each.

bestiary.notes: Adversarial verify done against Game8, ScreenRant/RankedBoost, GameRant, PowerPyx, Zelda Dungeon, Kiranico, Fextralife. Corrections made to the original dataset: (1) Stalnox drop "Ancient Bone" was wrong/invented; real drops are Stalnox Horn + Hinox Tooth. (2) Like Like listed "Sticky Frog" as a drop, which is invented (Sticky Frog is a critter caught near water, not a monster drop); corrected to Opal + chest gear, with Ice Like Stone/Rock Like Stone from variants. (3) Frox was missing its "Frox Fang" drop and listed vague "gems"; corrected to Frox Fang/Fingernail/Guts + Zonaite/Large Zonaite/Crystallized Charge. (4) Boss Bokoblin drop "Bokoblin Horn (high tier)" corrected to the real materials Boss Bokoblin Horn + Boss Bokoblin Guts. (5) Construct drop "Zonaite" corrected to "Zonai Charge" (the standard Construct drop). (6) Lynel "Lynel Horn" corrected to the real horn names Lynel Saber Horn / Mace Horn. (7) Phantom Ganon drop expanded to include the Gloom weapon (Sword/Club/Spear) it was wielding, alongside the always-dropped Demon King's Bow and Dark Clump. (8) Aerocuda tactic edited (Keese Eyeball-fused arrow homes in). (9) Gibdo tactic refined: they harden/revive and are broken by any element (fire/electric/ice), not specifically a fused Gibdo Bone. (10) Hinox drops expanded (Tooth also drops). (11) Stone Talus renamed to Luminous/Rare variants with accurate gem list. (12) The grouped "Seized/Marbled bosses" entry renamed to the actual four temple bosses. Verified-accurate as written (no change): Bokoblin, Moblin, Lizalfos, Horriblin, Molduga, all Gleeok elemental counters, Flux Construct weak-block, Frox eye/ore cycle, Demon Dragon (4 back weak spots, Master Sword 10x dmg, Light Dragon lifts you between hits), Gloom Hands light/Bomb-arrow tactic. Tiers kept as: common, mini-boss (field bosses), boss (region/major), construct (Zonai automata), gloom (gloom-type). Lower confidence: exact full drop tables are representative not exhaustive (higher-tier color variants add Guts and extra gems/Zonaite). All step/tactic/drop strings kept under 170 chars, plain text.

rules: One effect per dish: mixing two different effect ingredients (e.g. Spicy + Hasty) cancels both, leaving a plain meal that only heals.
Stack the same prefix to go further: more same-effect ingredients raise the tier (Spicy to Spicy to Hot-Footed style) and lengthen the timer.
Cook in a lit Cooking Pot. Holding ingredients and dropping them in a campfire or open flame does NOT make effect meals.
Elixirs need a critter plus a monster part: e.g. Hightail Lizard + monster part = Hasty Elixir. No critter or no monster part means no elixir.
Monster parts set elixir duration, not the effect. Tougher monster part = longer timer; the critter decides which effect you get.
Add a Dragon Horn shard (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light) to any elixir to max the timer to 30 minutes instantly.
Sundelions add the Sunny effect, the only way to cook back gloom-damaged (locked) hearts; Sun Pumpkin also gives Sunny.
Brightcaps and Glowing Cave Fish make Bright meals that give Link a glowing aura: hugely useful in the dark Depths and caves.
Muddle Buds and Dazzlefruit are throw/Fuse materials, not cooking ingredients: they confuse or blind enemies, they do not make meals.
Star fragments and most gems/ores can't be cooked; adding a non-food item usually produces Dubious Food with no effect.
Up to 5 ingredients per dish. Adding more of the same effect food gives more hearts/duration up to the cap.
Hearty-effect dishes fully heal and add temporary yellow (bonus) hearts; they ignore the normal heart count.

effects.does: Fully restores hearts and adds temporary bonus (yellow) hearts on top of your max.
effects.ingredients: Big Hearty Radish, Hearty Radish, Hearty Truffle, Big Hearty Truffle, Hearty Salmon, Hearty Bass.

effects.does: Instantly refills part of your stamina wheel for climbing, gliding, and sprinting.
effects.ingredients: Stamella Shroom, Stambulb, plus Energetic Rhino Beetle for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Energetic Rhino Beetle + any monster part = Energizing Elixir.

effects.does: Overfills stamina past full with extra green wheels (best for long climbs/glides).
effects.ingredients: Endura Carrot, Endura Shroom, plus Tireless Frog for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Tireless Frog + any monster part = Enduring Elixir.

effects.does: Keeps Link warm in cold regions so you don't take freezing chip damage.
effects.ingredients: Spicy Pepper, Sizzlefin Trout, plus Warm Darner for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Warm Darner + any monster part = Spicy Elixir.

effects.does: Stops heat damage in hot areas like the Gerudo Desert and Eldin's hot lowlands. Note: Death Mountain's extreme heat needs Fireproof, not this.
effects.ingredients: Hydromelon, Cool Safflina, Chillfin Trout, plus Cold Darner for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Cold Darner + any monster part = Chilly Elixir.

effects.does: Prevents Link bursting into flame in extreme heat (e.g. Death Mountain). Elixir only.
effects.ingredients: Fireproof Lizard or Smotherwing Butterfly, each plus any monster part.
effects.elixir: Fireproof Lizard (or Smotherwing Butterfly) + any monster part = Fireproof Elixir.

effects.does: Reduces or blocks electric/shock damage and stops weapons being knocked from your hand.
effects.ingredients: Voltfruit, Zapshroom, Electric Safflina, Voltfin Trout, plus Electric Darner for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Electric Darner + any monster part = Electro Elixir.

effects.does: Temporarily raises your attack power so weapons hit harder.
effects.ingredients: Mighty Bananas, Razorshroom, Razorclaw Crab, Mighty Carp, plus Bladed Rhino Beetle for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Bladed Rhino Beetle + any monster part = Mighty Elixir.

effects.does: Temporarily raises defense so you take less damage from hits.
effects.ingredients: Ironshroom, Fortified Pumpkin, Armored Carp, Ironshell Crab, plus Rugged Rhino Beetle for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Rugged Rhino Beetle + any monster part = Tough Elixir.

effects.does: Increases Link's movement and running speed on foot.
effects.ingredients: Swift Carrot, Fleet-Lotus Seeds, Swift Violet, Hyrule Bass, plus Hightail Lizard for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Hightail Lizard + any monster part = Hasty Elixir.

effects.does: Lowers the noise Link makes so enemies and animals are harder to alert.
effects.ingredients: Silent Princess, Blue Nightshade, Silent Shroom, Sneaky River Snail, plus Sunset Firefly for elixirs.
effects.elixir: Sunset Firefly + any monster part = Sneaky Elixir.

effects.does: Prevents slipping while climbing on wet surfaces or in the rain.
effects.ingredients: Sticky Lizard or Sticky Frog, each plus any monster part (elixir only).
effects.elixir: Sticky Lizard (or Sticky Frog) + any monster part = Sticky Elixir.

effects.does: Surrounds Link with a glowing aura that lights the area: vital in the dark Depths and caves.
effects.ingredients: Brightcap, Glowing Cave Fish; a simple Bright meal is Brightcap + Apple.
effects.elixir: Deep Firefly + any monster part = Bright Elixir.

effects.does: Heals normal hearts AND restores gloom-damaged (locked) hearts, which nothing else cooks back.
effects.ingredients: Sundelion (best), Sun Pumpkin; both give the Sunny effect.

recipes.name: Sunny Fried Wild Greens
recipes.makes: Restores gloom-damaged hearts (up to ~15 from Gloom)
recipes.why: Sun Pumpkin + Sundelion. The go-to before Depths runs and gloom-heavy fights, since Sunny is the only gloom cure.

recipes.name: Hearty Steamed Fish
recipes.makes: Full heal plus many temporary bonus hearts
recipes.why: Hearty Salmon or Hearty Bass alone in the pot. Tons of yellow hearts for tough bosses; stack more Hearty fish for more.

recipes.name: Enduring Carrot Stew
recipes.why: Endura Carrots only. Extra green stamina wheels make long climbs and sky-glides trivial; better than plain Energizing.

recipes.name: Mighty Crab Stir-Fry
recipes.why: Razorclaw Crab + Mighty ingredient + Goron Spice/Hylian herbs. Easy, high-tier attack boost for fights and the Depths.

recipes.name: Bright Meal (Brightcap + Apple)
recipes.makes: Glowing aura to light dark areas
recipes.why: Cheap, farmable Brightcaps. Lights your path in the Depths so you can navigate between Lightroots without flares.

recipes.name: Fireproof Elixir
recipes.makes: Flame Guard (no burning in extreme heat)
recipes.why: Fireproof Lizard or Smotherwing Butterfly + any monster part. Needed around Death Mountain before Goron/Fire content.

dragons.name: Dinraal
dragons.where: Circles Eldin and Akkala; enters Depths at Drenan Highlands Chasm, exits at East Akkala Plains Chasm. Catch from Ulri Mountain or Thyphlo Ruins Skyview Tower.
dragons.parts: Scale, Claw, Fang, Horn (shard), Spike. Don't use a wooden bow near it (fire). Fuse for fire weapons; Horn maxes elixir timer to 30 min.

dragons.name: Naydra
dragons.where: Emerges near Mount Lanayru, circles Necluda/Hateno, dives near East Hill above Kakariko. Easy farm from Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower.
dragons.parts: Scale, Claw, Fang, Horn (shard), Spike. She is freezing-cold, so bring cold resistance. Fuse for ice weapons; Horn maxes elixir timer.

dragons.name: Farosh
dragons.where: Circles Gerudo and Faron; enters Depths at Hills of Baumer Chasm, exits at East Gerudo Chasm. Wait at Popla Foothills Skyview Tower or Mount Hylia.
dragons.parts: Scale, Claw, Fang, Horn (shard), Spike. Don't use a metal bow near it (shock). Fuse for electric weapons; Horn maxes elixir timer.

dragons.name: Light Dragon
dragons.where: Circles the entire map at the far edges and a very high altitude; needs Sky islands/towers to reach. (Tied to the main story; spoiler-light here.)
dragons.parts: Scale, Claw, Fang, Horn (shard), Spike. Parts make powerful light/holy fuses and its Horn maxes elixir timers like the others.

cooking.notes: CORRECTIONS MADE (web-verified vs Game8, Zelda Dungeon, Gamer Rant, June 2026): (1) Removed 'Hearty Durian' from Hearty ingredients — it does NOT exist in TotK (it was a BotW item, cut from this game). Replaced with Big Hearty Truffle, a real TotK Hearty item. (2) Fixed Chilly 'does' line: removed 'Death Mountain foothills' as a Chilly use, since Death Mountain's extreme heat requires the Fireproof effect, not heat resistance — leaving this could send a first-timer to Death Mountain with the wrong dish and get them burned. (3) Corrected Dinraal's Depths entry to the proper in-game name 'Drenan Highlands Chasm' (was 'Drenan Highland Chasm') and clarified exit as 'East Akkala Plains Chasm'. Everything else verified ACCURATE: all effect/critter/elixir mappings (Energetic Rhino Beetle=Energizing, Tireless Frog=Enduring, Warm/Cold/Electric Darner, Hightail Lizard, Bladed/Rugged Rhino Beetle, Sunset Firefly, Deep Firefly=Bright Elixir, Fireproof Lizard/Smotherwing Butterfly, Sticky Lizard/Frog); Spicy=cold resist and Chilly=heat resist (correctly assigned, not swapped); the cooking rules (two effects cancel to a plain heal, same-prefix stacking, lit pot required, critter+monster part for elixirs, monster part sets duration, Dragon Horn shard = 30-min max for all four dragons including Light); Sundelion/Sun Pumpkin=Sunny gloom cure (Sundelion ~3 hearts each, Sun Pumpkin ~1); Brightcap/Glowing Cave Fish=Bright; Muddle Bud/Dazzlefruit being throw/Fuse not cooking; all four dragon routes and farming towers (Mount Lanayru, Ulri Mountain, Thyphlo Ruins, Popla Foothills towers all real). Lower confidence remains on the exact 'best' single critter per effect since many share an effect — listed ones are confirmed correct, just not guaranteed the absolute optimum. All steps kept under 170 chars, plain text, real in-game proper names. Light Dragon kept deliberately spoiler-aware.

systems.name: The three layers: Surface, Sky, Depths
systems.what: Hyrule has three stacked worlds. Sky has floating islands and Skyview Towers. The Depths sit beneath the Surface, dark and full of Gloom.

systems.name: The Depths and Gloom
systems.what: The Depths are pitch-black. Toss Brightbloom Seeds or hit Lightroots to light them. Gloom damages and locks hearts until you reach light or eat a sundelion dish.

systems.name: Lightroots mirror shrines
systems.what: Activating a Lightroot lights a chunk of the Depths and marks the map. All 120 Lightroots sit under the 120 Surface shrines, names spelled backwards.

systems.name: Poes and Bargainer Statues
systems.what: Poes are blue flames found in the Depths; they are currency. Spend them at the seven Bargainer Statues to buy Gloom-resistant gear and items.

systems.name: Shrines and Light of Blessing
systems.what: There are 152 shrines across Surface and Sky. Each gives a Light of Blessing. Trade 4 at any Goddess Statue for one heart or one stamina vessel.

systems.name: Heart and stamina swap
systems.what: At the Horned Statue in the bunker beneath Lookout Landing you can rebalance hearts and stamina. It costs rupees: sell an essence for 100, buy the other back for 120.

systems.name: Battery and Energy Cells
systems.what: Energy Cells power Zonai devices. Mine Zonaite in the Depths, trade 3 to a Forge Construct for a Crystallized Charge, then add cells at a Crystal Refinery.

systems.name: Skyview Towers
systems.what: 15 Skyview Towers reveal the regional map and launch you skyward. Open the shutters by solving each tower's small puzzle, then get fired into the Sky.

systems.name: Zonai devices and Autobuild
systems.what: Zonai devices (fans, wheels, rockets) attach with Ultrahand. Autobuild rebuilds saved or recent creations from materials, spending Zonaite for missing parts.

systems.name: Great Fairies
systems.what: Four Great Fairies (Tera, Cotera, Kaysa, Mija) upgrade armor. Unlock each by reuniting the Stable Trotters troupe, led by conductor Mastro, near a stable.

systems.name: Korok seeds and Hestu
systems.what: Find Korok seeds (900 total) and give them to Hestu to expand your weapon, bow, and shield pouches. Many hide behind carry-a-Korok-to-its-friend puzzles.

systems.name: Dragon's Tears and geoglyphs
systems.what: Visit the geoglyphs drawn across the Surface to unlock the Dragon's Tears memories, telling the Imprisoning War story out of order.

systems.name: Sage abilities and Sage's Will
systems.what: The five sages grant abilities you trigger by their avatar (Tulin gusts, Yunobo charges, etc.). Offer 4 Sage's Wills at a Goddess Statue to boost one sage (about 1.3x).

systems.name: The four temples and the fifth sage
systems.what: Wind (Rito/Tulin), Fire (Goron/Yunobo), Water (Zora/Sidon), Lightning (Gerudo/Riju). Mineru, the fifth sage, comes from the Spirit Temple.

upgrades: Hearts and stamina: collect Lights of Blessing from shrines (152 total) and trade 4 at a Goddess Statue for one heart container or one stamina vessel.
Heart/stamina swap: at the Horned Statue in the Lookout Landing bunker you can rebalance later. It is not free; each swap nets about 20 rupees (sell 100, rebuy 120).
Battery (Energy Cells): mine Zonaite in the Depths, trade 3 Zonaite per Crystallized Charge at a Forge Construct, then spend 100 charges per cell at a Crystal Refinery.
Pouch slots: hand Korok seeds to Hestu to add weapon, bow, and shield inventory slots; costs climb as you expand.
Armor: upgrade gear at any of the four Great Fairy fountains once unlocked, spending materials and rupees per level.
Sage power: collect Sage's Wills (20 exist; some guarded by Gleeoks) and offer 4 at a Goddess Statue to strengthen a chosen sage's ability about 1.3x.

fairies.name: Tera
fairies.location: Near Woodland Stable in Eldin, by Pico Pond. You meet conductor Mastro and the Stable Trotters here; awaken Tera first to unlock the others.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the Stable Trotters); armor upgrades then cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Cotera
fairies.location: Near Dueling Peaks Stable, south of Kakariko Village on the east side of Dueling Peaks.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the next Stable Trotters musician); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Kaysa
fairies.location: Near Outskirt Stable, southwest edge of Hyrule Field, west of the Coliseum Ruins.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the Stable Trotters); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Mija
