fairies.location: Near Snowfield Stable in the South Tabantha Snowfield (cold region; pack warm gear).
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (final Stable Trotters performance); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

world.notes: Web-verified against Nintendo Life, Game8, GameWith, ZeldaDungeon, Fextralife. CORRECTIONS to the source dataset: (1) The heart/stamina swap is done at the HORNED STATUE (also nicknamed the Cursed Statue), hidden in the Emergency Shelter bunker beneath Lookout Landing, unlocked via the "Who Goes There?!" quest. It is NOT a Goddess Statue and NOT a Bargainer/"Statue of the Dead" (those are the separate Poe-trading statues). (2) The swap is NOT free: you sell an essence for 100 rupees and rebuy the other type for 120, a net 20 rupees per swap. The source's own note that recommended dropping the Horned Statue reference was backwards and has been reversed. (3) Korok seed total corrected from 1000 to 900 (giving all to Hestu fully maxes pouches; the famous "1000th" is a meme/extra, but the real count is 900). (4) Softened "press A" sage-activation wording since activation differs per sage (Tulin gust on glide, Yunobo charge on aim+attack, etc.); walking up + prompt is the secondary cue. Confirmed accurate as given: 152 shrines, 120 Lightroots (names spelled backwards under Surface shrines), 15 Skyview Towers, 7 Bargainer Statues (1 Surface + 6 Depths), 20 Sage's Wills (some guarded by King Gleeoks), 4 Sage's Wills per upgrade at ~1.3x, 4 Lights of Blessing per vessel, 3 Zonaite per Crystallized Charge at a Forge Construct, 100 charges per Energy Cell at the Crystal Refinery, 4 Great Fairies (Tera, Cotera, Kaysa, Mija) unlocked via the Stable Trotters troupe led by conductor Mastro, Tera awakened first. Medium-high confidence on stable pairings; Tera-first ordering is firm. Note: Korok total of 900 is well documented but some count the bonus, so if your app already standardized on 900 keep it.

flags: Tally sums to 196, not 152 (both count fields and listed names agree at 196, so the overcount comes from duplicate rows, not arithmetic).
Only 144 distinct shrine names exist across all regions; even after dedup that is 8 short of the real 152 (120 Surface + 32 Sky).
DUPLICATE REGION: 'Gerudo' and 'Gerudo Wasteland' are the same area listed twice - all 19 shrines appear in both (17 unique + 2 sky), causing 17 double-counts. Delete one bucket.
OVERLAPPING LAYER: 'Sky Islands (other)' re-lists shrines also counted under surface regions, causing ~32 double-counts. A shrine must live in exactly one bucket.
FAKE REGION: 'Lake' bucket duplicates En-oma + Jiukoum (already in Faron) and Utojis (already in Necluda). Delete it (3 dupes).
TRIPLE-COUNTED: Mayasiar, Rakashog, Siyamotsus each appear 3x (Sky Islands + Gerudo + Gerudo Wasteland). Verified as Gerudo Sky shrines - keep once in the Sky bucket.
TUTORIAL SHRINES DOUBLE-LISTED: Ukouh, In-isa, Gutanbac, Nachoyah are in both Great Sky Island and Central Hyrule. Keep once under Great Sky Island.
SPELLING INCONSISTENCY: 'In-isa Shrine' vs 'In-Isa Shrine' - same shrine, differing capitalization. Canonical = 'In-isa Shrine'.

Cross-region duplicate names (48): Chichim, En-oma, Ga-ahisas, Ganos, Gikaku, Gutanbac, Igoshon, Ijo-o, Irasak, Jinodok, Jirutagumac, Jiukoum, Jogou, Joku-u, Joku-usin, Josiu, Kadaunar, Kahatanaum, Karahatag, Kitawak, Kudanisar, Kumamayn, Mayamats, Mayanas, Mayasiar, Mayatat, Mayaumekis, Miryotanog, Mogisari, Motsusis, Nachoyah, Natak, Otutsum, Rakakudaj, Rakashog, Rotsumamu, Sihajog, Siwakama, Siyamotsus, Soryotanog, Suariwak, Taninoud, Tenbez, Turakamik, Ukoojisi, Ukouh, Utojis, Yansamin.

FIXES: (1) make region buckets mutually exclusive; (2) delete 'Gerudo Wasteland'; (3) delete 'Lake'; (4) keep each sky shrine only in the Sky bucket, not also in a surface region; (5) normalize In-Isa to In-isa; (6) after dedup recount against the authoritative 120 Surface + 32 Sky list and add the 8 genuinely missing shrines.
audit.verdict: Does not reconcile. The tally sums to 196 (not 152) because of duplicate rows, not bad arithmetic - the count fields and listed names both total 196. Three overlap patterns cause all 52 over-counts: (1) 'Gerudo' and 'Gerudo Wasteland' are the same region listed twice (17 dupes); (2) 'Sky Islands (other)' re-lists shrines also counted in their surface regions, including the 4 Great Sky Island tutorial shrines and the triple-counted Mayasiar/Rakashog/Siyamotsus (~32 dupes); (3) a fake 'Lake' bucket duplicates 3 Faron/Necluda shrines. Plus a spelling slip (In-isa vs In-Isa). After dedup only 144 distinct names remain - still 8 short of the real 152 (120 Surface + 32 Sky, verified). Fix: make buckets mutually exclusive (one region per shrine; Depths has Lightroots not shrines), delete the Gerudo Wasteland and Lake buckets, keep each sky shrine only in the Sky layer, normalize In-isa, then recount and add the 8 missing shrines. Confidence high on arithmetic/duplicates, medium-high on canonical region assignments; the 8 missing shrines were not individually identified and must be cross-checked before shipping.
